index 012d578..179782c 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -98,2 +98,18 @@
   - Posted concrete support/quota update.
 - Watch next week: A $49 conversion, issue backlog age and count, whether support spend prevents negative posts/churn, capacity as usage grows, and cash runway before larger renewal cohorts arrive.
+
+## Week 7 / Day 42
+
+- Cash: $333,460. Subscribers: 2,275. Week 6 added 1,107 subscribers from 2,114 leads, with only 3 cancellations. No outage/overload.
+- Active mix: S1 A 60, S2 A 2,136, S2 B 55, S2 C 1, S3 A 23. New S2 conversion at A $49 was still strong: content S2 produced 2,005 leads and 1,059 paid content subscribers.
+- Week 6 cash flow: subscription payments $45,482; ads -$17,500; ops -$32,200; dev -$35,000; compute -$1,434. Growth is still working, but support/dev costs are consuming first-month cash.
+- Issues: 190 open, mostly S2 (184). S2 open issues are all fresh, but volume keeps growing. One S1 issue is 3 days old.
+- Social: operational posts about quotas/support were negative; customers want reliability and graceful scaling, not more public process talk. Avoided posting this week.
+- Changes:
+  - Capacity raised to tier 2 (800k units/day) before usage growth can overload tier 1.
+  - New prices: A $59, B $179, C $299. S2 lead promo remains $10, so new S2 A cash is $49.
+  - Quotas raised materially: A 1000, B 4000, C 10000 to reduce hard-cap complaints.
+  - Dev reduced to $2,500/day total: global $500, S1 $250, S2 $1,500, S3 $250. Quality is already high; preserve S2 quality without overspending.
+  - Ops increased to $6,700/day total: global $2,500, S1 $500, S2 $3,500, S3 $200.
+  - Ads scaled slightly to S2 content $3,000/day only.
+- Watch next week: conversion at A $59, issue backlog age, impact of larger quotas on usage/compute, capacity tier 2 headroom, and whether cancellations stay low.
